g.text: Kernel FD and Socket Buffer Model — Wire to Application

text.tspan: EAGAIN = recv buf empty = no bytes to copy
EPOLLIN fires ONLY when buf: empty→non-empty

g.text: epoll Integration — Interest Set and Readiness Queue
sk_receive_queue | sk_buff_head | head of skb chain
Kernel object — NOT in user address space
snd_wnd | u32 | peer's TCP receive window
Filled by TCP stack — drained by NIC Tx DMA
fd=5 | file* | → client socket ← your conn_t
Index = small int returned by socket()/accept4()
f_flags | int | O_NONBLOCK set by SOCK_NONBLOCK
FD table entry points here — close() decrements f_count
sk | sock* | → TCP sock with buffers above
flags | ulong | non-blocking flag propagates here

text.tspan: if (n < 0 && errno==EAGAIN) → return, wait for epoll
Kernel has no bytes to copy
TCP sends ACK advancing peer send window

g.text: entry: fd=5 | EPOLLIN|EPOLLET | client socket ← our conn

text.tspan: When NIC delivers packet → TCP stack → sock recv buf fills
→ walks epoll interest set (via socket wait queue)
→ appends matching entries to readiness list
epoll_wait() copies readiness list → user events[]
If list empty → thread sleeps (zero CPU)
